l1: Question 1 — When did the storage pool issue first appear?
Question 2 — Is the pool composed of physical disks or virtual disks?
Question 3 — Are all disks in the pool affected or only some?
Question 4 — Has the pool been recently expanded or modified?
Question 5 — Is the issue impacting production workloads or test environments?
l2: Advanced question 1 — Are there any disks showing warning or retired status in the pool?
Advanced question 2 — Has the server undergone hardware or firmware updates recently?
Advanced question 3 — Is the pool configured with parity, mirror, or simple layout?
Advanced question 4 — Are there tiered storage spaces (SSD + HDD) involved?
Advanced question 5 — Is the pool part of a clustered Storage Spaces Direct (S2D) setup?

l1: Command or question 1 — Open Server Manager and check Storage Pools status
Command or question 2 — Run PowerShell Get-StoragePool to confirm health
Command or question 3 — Verify physical disks with Get-PhysicalDisk
Command or question 4 — Check if virtual disks are online in Disk Management
Command or question 5 — Review Event Viewer for storage-related warnings
Command or question 6 — Run PowerShell Get-StorageTier to check tier health and usage
l2: Advanced command 1 — Run Get-VirtualDisk to check operational status
Advanced command 2 — Use Get-StorageJob to identify pending repair or rebuild jobs
Advanced command 3 — Query cluster logs if using Storage Spaces Direct
Advanced command 4 — Run Get-PhysicalDisk | ft FriendlyName,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus
Advanced command 5 — Check tiered storage performance counters

l1: Resolution step 1 — Restart the server and recheck pool health
Resolution step 2 — Reconnect or replace any missing physical disks
Resolution step 3 — Run Repair-VirtualDisk if a disk shows degraded status
Resolution step 4 — Ensure all disks are online and not set to retired
Resolution step 5 — Verify sufficient free capacity in the pool
l2: Advanced fix 1 — Run Reset-PhysicalDisk on disks stuck in retired state
Advanced fix 2 — Update firmware and drivers for storage controllers
Advanced fix 3 — Rebalance or re-tier storage spaces if performance degraded
Advanced fix 4 — Run cluster validation tests for Storage Spaces Direct
Advanced fix 5 — Escalate to Microsoft support if pool metadata corruption suspected

l1: Export log X — Save Event Viewer storage logs
Collect trace Y — Run Get-StoragePool and export results
Run command Z — Execute Get-PhysicalDisk and save output
Step 4 — Capture screenshot of Server Manager storage pool view
Step 5 — Collect OS build and patch level
Step 6 — Export Get-StorageTier results for tier health and usage
l2: Advanced collection 1 — Export Get-VirtualDisk detailed report
Advanced collection 2 — Collect cluster logs if using S2D
Advanced collection 3 — Gather performance monitor counters for storage spaces
Advanced collection 4 — Export Get-StorageJob status and history
Advanced collection 5 — Collect firmware/driver versions for all storage controllers

symptom_slug.title: Storage pool degraded or disks missing

steps.action: Run Get-StoragePool to confirm degraded state
steps.expect: Pool shows warning or degraded status

steps.action: Run Get-PhysicalDisk to identify unhealthy disks
steps.expect: One or more disks show retired or warning state
Disks return to healthy state and pool rebuilds
All disks and pool show healthy operational status
